description: Baseline: measured current core RuboCop autocorrect gap after autoresearch scaffolding.

asi.hypothesis: Establish a deterministic baseline metric before implementing any new cop autocorrections.
asi.next_action_hint: Implement autocorrect for a low-risk token replacement cop to reduce missing count by 1.

description: Add autocorrect for Style/EndBlock by rewriting END keyword to at_exit and add corrected fixture coverage.

asi.hypothesis: Simple keyword replacement cops can provide low-risk +1 improvements quickly.
asi.learning: RuboCop parity for this cop is a direct keyword replacement (`END` -> `at_exit`) with no structural rewrite needed.
asi.next_action_hint: Target another low-complexity token-level cop (e.g., Lint/BigDecimalNew).

description: Implement autocorrect for Lint/BigDecimalNew by removing `.new` (and leading `::` for cbase) plus corrected fixture tests.

asi.hypothesis: Small method-token removals are a reliable way to add parity quickly.
asi.learning: Using selector range removal (`.new`) works for standard calls; cbase (`::BigDecimal`) also needs `::` removal to match RuboCop output.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with single-selector replacement/remove cops in Style/Lint for incremental -1 improvements.

description: Add autocorrect for Style/StderrPuts by rewriting receiver+selector (`$stderr.puts` / `STDERR.puts`) to `warn`, with corrected fixture coverage.

asi.hypothesis: Receiver+selector replacement cops are another low-risk class for fast parity gains.
asi.learning: Replacing range [send start..selector end] with `warn` preserves argument syntax for both parenthesized and non-parenthesized calls.
asi.next_action_hint: Look for additional one-range replacements (e.g., call-name swaps) before tackling multi-edit layout cops.

description: Implement autocorrect for Style/RedundantCurrentDirectoryInPath by removing leading `./+` from require_relative string literals and add corrected fixture tests.

asi.hypothesis: String-prefix removal cops can be added with simple byte-range deletions.
asi.learning: Removing `./+` from quoted string content via byte offsets matches existing fixtures and preserves surrounding quotes.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep targeting Style/Lint cops with single-range substitutions before moving to layout/indentation transforms.

description: Add autocorrect for Style/ArrayJoin by rewriting `array * string` calls to `array.join(string)` and add corrected fixture tests.

asi.hypothesis: Full-node replacement is effective for simple operator-to-method migrations.
asi.learning: Replacing the full call with `<receiver_source>.join(<arg_source>)` matches fixture behavior and avoids partial-token edge cases.
asi.next_action_hint: Prioritize remaining Style cops that can be expressed as whole-call rewrites or simple selector swaps.

description: Implement autocorrect for Style/ArrayCoercion by rewriting `[*expr]` to `Array(expr)` and add corrected fixture tests.

asi.hypothesis: Single-splat array coercion cases can be safely converted with whole-node replacement.
asi.learning: For bracketed one-element arrays with splat, replacing full node with `Array(<splat_expr_source>)` matches current detection scope and fixture behavior.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue harvesting low-complexity Style cops using source-preserving whole-node replacements.

description: Add autocorrect for Style/EnvHome by replacing `ENV['HOME']`/`ENV.fetch('HOME', nil)` forms with `Dir.home` (leaving index-or-write cases uncorrected).

asi.hypothesis: Direct expression replacement cops can still autocorrect partially even when some matched node shapes are intentionally non-correctable.
asi.learning: Safe rewrite is straightforward for call-node forms; keeping `ENV['HOME'] ||= ...` unchanged avoids invalid assignment targets while still enabling autocorrect support.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with cops where the offense range is the whole expression and replacement is a stable literal/template.

description: Implement autocorrect for Lint/IdentityComparison by rewriting `lhs.object_id ==/!= rhs.object_id` to `lhs.equal?(rhs)` / `!lhs.equal?(rhs)`, with corrected fixture coverage and research log update.

asi.hypothesis: Object-id comparison cops can be converted with receiver extraction and whole-expression replacement.
asi.learning: Using inner `.object_id` receiver source for both sides preserves ivar/lvar forms and yields clean `equal?` rewrites for both `==` and `!=`.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue targeting call-rewrite cops in Lint/Style before addressing layout-family autocorrections.

description: Add autocorrect for Style/Dir (`File.expand_path(File.dirname(__FILE__))`/`File.dirname(File.realpath(__FILE__))` -> `__dir__`), Style/SymbolLiteral (`:"foo"` -> `:foo`), and Style/FileNull (null-device string -> `File::NULL`) with corrected fixtures.

asi.hypothesis: Batching several low-complexity whole-node rewrites should produce a larger real metric drop per iteration without risky parser changes.
asi.learning: The dominant fast-path remains single replacement edits over offense node ranges; visitor-based cops like FileNull can autocorrect by collecting offense ranges then emitting replacements post-traversal.
asi.test_scope: cargo test --lib -- cop::style::dir && cargo test --lib -- cop::style::symbol_literal && cargo test --lib -- cop::style::file_null
asi.next_action_hint: Continue mining short Style/Lint cops with one replace/remove operation before tackling Layout indentation-family corrections.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/ColonMethodCall (`::` method call operator -> `.`) and Style/ClassCheck (selector rewrite between `kind_of?`/`is_a?`) with corrected fixture tests.

asi.hypothesis: Selector-level rewrites in method-call cops are high-confidence, low-risk additions that scale quickly.
asi.learning: Using exact selector/operator locations (`call_operator_loc`, `message_loc`) yields stable autocorrects without disturbing arguments/receiver formatting.
asi.test_scope: cargo test --lib -- cop::style::colon_method_call && cargo test --lib -- cop::style::class_check
asi.next_action_hint: Keep harvesting selector/operator rewrite cops in Style and simple whole-node rewrite cops in Lint.

description: Add autocorrect for Security/JSONLoad (selector rewrite `load`/`restore` -> `parse`) and Lint/RegexpAsCondition (append ` =~ $_` to implicit regexp conditions) with corrected fixture tests.

asi.hypothesis: Simple selector and expression-template rewrites across Security/Lint can continue reducing the parity gap steadily.
asi.learning: For selector-focused cops, replacing only `message_loc` gives stable corrections; for match-current-line nodes, full-node replacement template (`<regexp> =~ $_`) matches RuboCop intent.
asi.test_scope: cargo test --lib -- cop::security::json_load && cargo test --lib -- cop::lint::regexp_as_condition
asi.next_action_hint: Prioritize remaining Style/Lint cops with either selector swaps or one-shot full-expression templates before moving to structural/layout autocorrects.

description: Enable autocorrect for Security/IoMethods (replace `IO` receiver with `File`) and Lint/TopLevelReturnWithArgument (rewrite `return <arg>` to `return`) with corrected fixture coverage.

asi.hypothesis: Remaining security/lint gaps with direct receiver or full-node rewrites can be closed quickly without touching detection logic.
asi.learning: Receiver-only replacement (`IO` -> `File`) preserves method args cleanly; visitor-based source cops can autocorrect by storing offense ranges and applying deterministic replacements post-traversal.
asi.test_scope: cargo test --lib -- cop::security::io_methods && cargo test --lib -- cop::lint::top_level_return_with_argument
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with Style/Lint cops that expose selector/operator locations for minimal-risk edits.

description: Add autocorrect for Lint/OrAssignmentToConstant (`||=` -> `=`) and Style/PercentQLiterals (`%Q` <-> `%q` opener rewrite per configured style) with corrected fixture coverage.

asi.hypothesis: Operator-token and percent-literal opener rewrites are low-friction additions that retain source shape while closing parity gaps.
asi.learning: Direct `operator_loc` replacement works for Prism *OrWrite nodes; for percent literals, replacing just the two-byte `%Q/%q` opener is sufficient and robust for current fixture scope.
asi.test_scope: cargo test --lib -- cop::lint::or_assignment_to_constant && cargo test --lib -- cop::style::percent_q_literals
asi.next_action_hint: Target more selector/operator rewrite cops and avoid deep AST restructuring until low-hanging fruit is exhausted.

description: Implement autocorrect for Style/StringMethods (selector rewrite to preferred method, default `intern` -> `to_sym`) and Style/ObjectThen (selector rewrite `yield_self` <-> `then` per enforced style), with corrected fixtures.

asi.hypothesis: Config-driven selector replacement cops can be converted rapidly using `message_loc` edits while preserving call structure.
asi.learning: For style cops that only differ by method name preference, replacing selector bytes is enough and avoids complex AST rewrites.
asi.test_scope: cargo test --lib -- cop::style::string_methods && cargo test --lib -- cop::style::object_then
asi.next_action_hint: Continue on style cops with message-level replacements (`intern`-like, alias method preference, keyword style toggles).

description: Add autocorrect for Lint/ToJSON (insert `(*_args)` on zero-arity `to_json` defs) and Style/BarePercentLiterals (rewrite `%Q` opener to bare `%` / `%` to `%Q` per style) with corrected fixtures.

asi.hypothesis: Method-signature insertion and percent-literal opener rewrites are robust, low-risk transformations for remaining Lint/Style gaps.
asi.learning: `name_loc` insertion works cleanly for def autocorrect (`to_json`); for percent literals, editing only the opener token preserves delimiters/content and matches fixture expectations.
asi.test_scope: cargo test --lib -- cop::lint::to_json && cargo test --lib -- cop::style::bare_percent_literals
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with style cops where corrections are pure selector/opener edits and avoid multiline structural rewrites until necessary.

description: Implement autocorrect for Style/PreferredHashMethods (selector rewrite between short/verbose hash predicates) and Style/ArrayIntersectWithSingleElement (`intersect?([x])` -> `include?(x)`) with corrected fixtures.

asi.hypothesis: Config-aware selector rewrites and small paired edits (selector + argument simplification) remain high-yield targets.
asi.learning: For `intersect?([elem])`, two non-overlapping edits (method selector and array-arg node) are enough for faithful correction without full-call reconstruction.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep targeting style cops where correction can be expressed as one or two localized range edits.

description: Add autocorrect for Style/DefWithParentheses (remove empty def parens) and Style/MethodDefParentheses (add/remove method parameter parens per style) with corrected fixture coverage.

asi.hypothesis: Definition-signature cops can be corrected with localized delimiter edits on `def` nodes.
asi.learning: For adding method parens, replacing the whitespace between method name and first param with `(` avoids `def foo (x)` spacing artifacts; delimiter-only edits remain stable.
asi.test_scope: cargo test --lib -- cop::style::def_with_parentheses && cargo test --lib -- cop::style::method_def_parentheses
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with syntax-style cops where corrections are insertion/removal of small delimiter tokens around existing source ranges.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/SingleArgumentDig (`obj.dig(x)` -> `obj[x]`) and Style/RedundantArrayConstructor (`Array.new([...])`/`Array[...]`/`Array([...])` simplification) with corrected fixtures.

asi.hypothesis: Whole-call replacement cops with clear canonical output are still strong low-risk opportunities.
asi.learning: For redundant constructor patterns, replacing the full call with source-derived argument forms (`[...]` or literal array arg) is deterministic and fixture-stable.

asi.next_action_hint: Proceed with other expression-level simplification cops before entering heavy layout indentation work.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/EmptyLambdaParameter (remove empty lambda parens) and Style/EmptyBlockParameter (remove empty block pipes) with corrected fixtures.

asi.hypothesis: Parameter-delimiter removal cops are low-risk full-range deletions that should safely increase autocorrectable overlap.
asi.learning: Using BlockParametersNode location with optional preceding-space trim produced stable corrections for both `-> () {}` and `do || ... end`/`{ || ... }` forms.

asi.next_action_hint: Continue with small Style/Lint cops that are operator/selector replacements before attempting complex Layout alignment cops.

description: Add autocorrect for Style/SuperWithArgsParentheses (insert missing parens), Lint/EmptyEnsure (remove empty ensure keyword), and Style/HashLookupMethod (fetch<->[] rewrites) with corrected fixtures.

asi.hypothesis: Simple token/range edits on existing parsers can safely unlock another small batch of RuboCop-core autocorrectable cops without changing detection scope.
asi.learning: Super paren insertion works reliably by replacing keyword->first-arg gap with '(' and inserting ')' after last arg/block pass; empty ensure aligns with RuboCop by removing only `ensure` keyword; hash lookup conversion is stable via full-call replacement.
asi.test_scope: cargo test --lib -- cop::style::super_with_args_parentheses && cargo test --lib -- cop::lint::empty_ensure && cargo test --lib -- cop::style::hash_lookup_method
asi.next_action_hint: Continue targeting low-branching Style/Lint cops around symbol/string/operator rewrites before moving into high-risk Layout alignment autocorrects.

description: Enable autocorrect for Lint/BooleanSymbol, including standalone `:true`/`:false` and keyword-hash `true:`/`false:` conversion to rocket form.

asi.hypothesis: Boolean-symbol replacements are deterministic and safe to implement as direct key/symbol range rewrites.
asi.learning: Using two correction modes matches RuboCop semantics: standalone symbols become boolean literals, while keyword hash labels become `:true =>` / `:false =>` to preserve symbol-key behavior.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with low-risk style cops requiring quote/operator swaps (e.g., QuotedSymbols or RedundantPercentQ with strict guardrails).

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/RedundantPercentQ by replacing redundant `%q/%Q` string literals with quoted string literals under strict safety guards.

asi.hypothesis: Redundant percent-literal offenses can be autocorrected safely when interpolation/escape-sensitive content is excluded.
asi.learning: Whole-node replacement to `'...'` or `"..."` works for current offense patterns; keeping backslash/interpolation guards avoids semantic drift.
asi.next_action_hint: Pursue another low-complexity Style cop (likely quote/operator rewrite) while maintaining strict correction guards to avoid overfitting.

description: Add autocorrect for Style/QuotedSymbols by normalizing quoted symbol delimiters for standalone symbols and hash-key symbols when conversion is safe.

asi.hypothesis: Quoted symbol offenses can be fixed via direct delimiter swaps while preserving content and key-vs-standalone form.
asi.learning: Replacing full symbol ranges with reconstructed quoted forms (`:'x'`, `'x':`, and double-quoted inverses) passes fixtures when interpolation/escape guards remain strict.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with similarly localized literal/operator cops; postpone layout-indent transformations until simple style/lint options are exhausted.

description: Enable autocorrect for Lint/UriRegexp by rewriting `URI.regexp` calls to `URI::DEFAULT_PARSER.make_regexp` (including qualified `::URI`).

asi.hypothesis: `URI.regexp` replacements are straightforward full-call rewrites and should be low-risk parity wins.
asi.learning: Receiver-preserving full replacement (`URI` or `::URI`) with optional first argument preserved matches existing fixture expectations.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with low-branching lint/style literal/operator rewrites; avoid structural transformation cops until easy wins flatten out.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/ConcatArrayLiterals by rewriting `receiver.concat([..], [..])` to `receiver.push(..)` with flattened array elements.

asi.hypothesis: `concat` calls whose arguments are all array literals can be safely canonicalized to `push` with element arguments.
asi.learning: Full-call replacement with flattened array element sources produced stable fixture-correct output for single and multiple array arguments.
asi.next_action_hint: Target additional simple literal/argument-shape cops in Style/Lint before starting Layout alignment corrections.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/MinMax by replacing `[receiver.min, receiver.max]` array literals with `receiver.minmax`.

asi.hypothesis: Min/max pair arrays are canonicalizable via a direct whole-range replacement to `.minmax`.
asi.learning: Replacing the full offending array node with `{receiver}.minmax` is stable for current fixture patterns and keeps diagnostics unchanged.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue harvesting low-risk style/lint canonicalization cops; keep layout cops deferred until simple wins are exhausted.

description: Enable autocorrect for Lint/PercentSymbolArray by removing unnecessary leading `:` and trailing `,` inside `%i/%I` elements.

asi.hypothesis: Element-local deletions on `%i/%I` literals should safely match RuboCop behavior for colon/comma cleanup.
asi.learning: Applying per-element leading/trailing token removals yields expected normalization; corrected fixture needed `%i[name role status]` (not colon-prefixed elements) for `%i[]` form.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with conservative Lint canonicalization cops; avoid ambiguous-expression rewrites unless correction boundaries are unambiguous.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/RedundantDoubleSplatHashBraces by replacing `**{...}` keyword-hash splats with direct keyword arguments.

asi.hypothesis: For non-empty symbol-key hash literals inside keyword splats, removing `**{}` wrapper is a safe canonicalization.
asi.learning: Using first/last hash element ranges to rebuild replacement preserves argument ordering and commas while avoiding hash-rocket and empty-hash edge cases.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep targeting simple keyword/argument literal cops before moving to high-complexity layout or parser-warning-driven rewrites.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/RedundantEach by removing chained redundant `.each` before `each`/`each_with_index`/`each_with_object` calls.

asi.hypothesis: Redundant chained enumerator `.each` calls can be corrected by removing the inner `.each` selector/operator span only.
asi.learning: Deleting the inner call-operator + selector range preserved surrounding chain behavior and matched fixture expectations for `each`, `each_with_index`, and `each_with_object`.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep harvesting small chain/literal canonicalization cops; defer high-risk layout alignment autocorrect work.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/UnpackFirst by converting `.unpack(...).first`/`[0]`/`at(0)`/`slice(0)` into `.unpack1(...)` and removing first-element access.

asi.hypothesis: Unpack-first chains can be safely corrected via selector swap (`unpack`->`unpack1`) plus tail-range removal.
asi.learning: Two precise edits (selector replacement + remove range from unpack-call end to outer-call end) handled both single-line and multiline chain fixtures.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with similarly localized chain canonicalizations; keep ambiguous parser-warning cops gated behind stricter safety checks.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/LambdaCall by rewriting implicit `lambda.(...)` calls to explicit `lambda.call(...)` in default call style.

asi.hypothesis: Lambda call-style offenses are safe full-call rewrites when receiver/operator/argument text is preserved.
asi.learning: Reconstructing full call text from receiver + operator + args allows deterministic conversion from implicit call syntax to explicit `call` without touching detection logic.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with style chain canonicalization cops that can be fixed via selector/range rewrites; keep ambiguous parser-warning cops conservative.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/RedundantArrayFlatten by removing redundant `.flatten` (and optional depth arg) before `.join`/`.join(nil)`.

asi.hypothesis: Redundant flatten-before-join patterns are safe to autocorrect via direct removal of the `.flatten(...)` span.
asi.learning: Deleting from flatten call-operator start through flatten call end correctly normalized `x.flatten.join`, `x.flatten.join(nil)`, and `x.flatten(1).join` fixture cases.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with one-range chain simplification cops (e.g., map/set or collection canonicalizations) before layout-focused work.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/MapToSet by rewriting `map/collect { ... }.to_set` to `to_set { ... }` and removing trailing `.to_set`.

asi.hypothesis: Map-to-set chains can be corrected with two localized edits: selector swap on inner call and removal of outer `.to_set`.
asi.learning: Using a paired correction (inner method name -> `to_set`, then deleting outer call operator+selector) preserves both block and symbol block-pass forms.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with collection canonicalization cops that can be expressed as selector/range edits before entering layout-heavy territory.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/InPatternThen by replacing semicolon separator in single-line `in pattern; body` clauses with ` then`.

asi.hypothesis: In-pattern semicolon offenses can be fixed with a one-character range replacement (`;` -> ` then`) under existing multiline/then guards.
asi.learning: Using detected semicolon byte offset for targeted replacement produced stable autocorrect across literal, tuple, and match-alt pattern fixtures.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with localized token/selector rewrites in Style; defer structural multiline/layout transformations.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/RedundantConstantBase by removing leading top-level `::` from constant paths where the cop already reports redundancy.

asi.hypothesis: Top-level redundant constant-base offenses are safe to fix by deleting the initial `::` token.

asi.next_action_hint: Continue with similarly local token rewrites in remaining Style/Lint cops; postpone layout and no-op Ruby-version cops.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/RedundantRegexpConstructor by replacing `Regexp.new(/.../)` and `Regexp.compile(/.../)` with direct regexp literals.

asi.hypothesis: Constructor-to-literal regexp rewrites are safe when exactly one regexp-literal argument is present.
asi.learning: Whole-call replacement with the original regexp argument source produced correct output for plain and optioned literals (e.g., `/baz/i`) while preserving guarded exclusions (extra args).
asi.next_action_hint: Keep targeting Style canonicalization cops with whole-expression or token-level rewrites; defer risky multiline/layout transforms.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/CommandLiteral by converting `%x(...)` command literals to backtick form when inner-backtick-safe.

asi.hypothesis: Delimiter-only rewrites on command literals are safe for default backticks style when inner content has no backticks.
asi.learning: Replacing opening and closing delimiter ranges (`%x`-form -> backticks) yielded stable corrections for current offense fixtures while preserving conservative inner-backtick guards.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with token/selector-style cops that avoid multiline structural rewrites; keep ambiguous expression transformations conservative.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/DoubleCopDisableDirective by merging multiple same-line `rubocop:disable`/`rubocop:todo` directives into a single comma-separated directive.

asi.hypothesis: Line-local disable-directive cleanup is safe as a pure comment-text rewrite starting at the first directive token.
asi.learning: Replacing the comment segment from first directive to line end with merged comma-separated directives matched fixtures for both disable and todo forms.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep prioritizing style cops with deterministic token-level rewrites; avoid uncertain parser-warning autocorrections unless bounded.

description: Enable autocorrect for Lint/TrailingCommaInAttributeDeclaration by removing the trailing comma token in attr_* declarations that swallow following method defs.

asi.hypothesis: Trailing-comma attr declaration offenses can be corrected with a single-token removal at the detected comma offset.
asi.learning: Using the scanned comma position after the last real attr arg produced precise comma-only removals and preserved surrounding formatting/body lines.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with similarly local Style/Lint rewrites; avoid broad structural rewrites unless fixture coverage is expanded first.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/WhileUntilDo by deleting the `do` segment between multiline loop conditions and loop bodies.

asi.hypothesis: Multiline `while/until ... do` offenses can be corrected by removing the range from condition end to `do` keyword end.
asi.learning: Using predicate end + `do_keyword_loc` produced stable corrections across wrapped predicates, inline comments, and one-line body forms while preserving guardrails.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/BlockComments by rewriting `=begin...=end` blocks into `#` line comments while preserving inner content lines.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/EmptyHeredoc by replacing empty heredoc literals with empty string literals.

asi.hypothesis: Empty heredoc offenses are safely fixable by replacing the full heredoc range (opening through closing delimiter) with `''`.
asi.learning: Using opening_loc start and closing_loc end gave deterministic whole-node replacements; fixture output normalizes adjacent blank lines accordingly.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/RedundantInterpolationUnfreeze by replacing redundant `+@`/`dup` wrappers around interpolated strings with the interpolated string expression itself.

asi.hypothesis: Redundant unfreeze wrappers on interpolated strings can be safely autocorrected by replacing the full call node with its interpolated-string receiver.
asi.learning: Whole-call replacement using receiver source handled both regular and heredoc interpolation forms in fixtures while respecting Ruby >= 3.0 guardrails.

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/VariableInterpolation by converting `#$var`/`#@ivar`/`#@@cvar` interpolation forms to braced expressions (`#{$var}`, etc.).

description: Enable autocorrect for Lint/RescueType by removing invalid rescue exception types from rescue clauses (falling back to bare `rescue` when none remain valid).

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/PerlBackrefs by replacing Perl-style backrefs and English aliases with `Regexp.last_match` expressions (including namespace-aware `::` prefix).

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/RedundantRegexpEscape by removing redundant regexp escape backslashes while preserving meaningful escapes (char-class context and delimiter cases).

description: Enable autocorrect for Style/ArrayIntersect by rewriting supported intersection checks (`(a & b).any?/empty?/none?` and `a.intersection(b).any?/empty?/none?`) to `intersect?` equivalents.
